content: codex-claude-bridge documents and recommends exactly two models: `gpt-5.5` as the default (current flagship) and `gpt-5.4` as the documented fallback. This is a RECOMMENDATION boundary — it scopes the README model table, error-message tips, CLI help text, and any new default choices. It is NOT an enforcement gate. If a user explicitly sets a different model via `.reviewbridge.json`, the MCP `model` parameter, or the `--model` CLI flag, we forward it to Codex as-is. We don't advertise older Codex variants (`gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`) or mini models, but we also don't block users from running them. Tension to respect: curate our recommendations; don't gate user autonomy.
context: Initial policy captured 2026-04-23 after README cleanup (commit d9da0c2). A stricter enforcement pass landed in 0.3.4 (commit ef803d4) that rejected non-allowlisted models at config parse time. User pushback on that release: "we shouldnt offer it ourselves (5.1) but if user chooses it we shouldnt block them." Reverted in 0.3.5 — `config.model` is `z.string().default('gpt-5.5')` again, `RECOMMENDED_MODELS` retained as a documentation constant for tip/help-text sourcing but not used as a schema refine. Use this distinction when adding new config fields: curate defaults + docs, but keep inputs permissive unless a value is categorically unsafe (e.g. would crash the process or leak secrets). Supported set grows by editing this lesson + README + constant, not by shipping new enum values.
title: Recommend gpt-5.5 + gpt-5.4 only; never block the user's model choice
